commit_frequency_per_day.all_time: ~44 commits/day (1613 commits in ~37 days since repo created May 2, 2026)

message_format_analysis.pr_references: all commits reference a PR number in the subject line
message_format_analysis.message_quality: High — all messages are unique, descriptive, and specific. No filler or repeated messages detected.
message_format_analysis.body_detail: Most commits include multi-paragraph bodies explaining rationale, implementation approach, and sub-bullet details.

vibe_coding_signals: AI CO-AUTHORSHIP: Multiple commits by vanceingalls contain 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>' in commit body. Also found on jrusso1020 commits (e.g., 4da567d). This is a direct indicator of AI-generated code being committed.

BURST COMMIT PATTERN (vanceingalls): 7 commits in ~15 minutes on June 7, 2026 (19:07:59 → 19:23:27 Pacific). Four of these landed within 80 seconds (19:22:07, 19:22:34, 19:23:06, 19:23:27). This pattern is consistent with squash-merging multiple AI-generated branches in rapid succession.

EXTREME PR VELOCITY: PR numbers reached #1274 in ~37 days of repo existence (~34.4 PRs/day average). Combined with AI co-authorship, this suggests a high-volume AI-assisted development workflow where branches are generated and merged at a rate impossible for unaided human developers.

SINGLE-AUTHOR DOMINANCE: vanceingalls authors ~53% of recent commits, with miguel-heygen at ~37%. The vanceingalls pattern shows AI co-authorship; miguel-heygen shows no AI co-authorship signals, suggesting a human-led core with AI-assisted feature work.

SEQUENTIAL BRANCH MERGING: Many commits share near-identical timestamps with sequential PR numbers (e.g., #1257→#1258→#1259→#1261→#1262→#1263→#1267→#1268 all merged within 50 minutes on June 7). This is a classic vibe coding pattern: AI generates features in parallel branches, human reviews and squash-merges them in rapid succession.

notes: Repository created ~May 2, 2026 — all 1613 commits fall within approximately 37 days. This is an extraordinarily high commit velocity (~44/day). The repository uses squash-merging into main, resulting in one commit per PR. All but one commit in the sample set have the author field matching the GitHub user, with GitHub (noreply@github.com) as committer — confirming a PR-based squash-merge workflow. miguel-heygen commits show zero AI co-authorship signals and appear to be human-authored core infrastructure work. vanceingalls commits frequently carry 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6' — a strong and direct vibe coding indicator. The project appears to be a legitimate AI-assisted development workflow with human review gates (PR squash merges), not a fully automated bot pipeline.
